title: C++ Programming Basics
description: Variables, data types, operators, and basic syntax

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax to output 'Hello World' in C++?
questions.explanation: In C++, cout is used with the insertion operator (<<) to output text to the console.

questions.question: What is the correct way to declare a variable in C++?
questions.explanation: Variables in C++ are declared by specifying the data type followed by the variable name.

questions.question: Which header file is required for input/output operations in C++?
questions.explanation: The <iostream> header file provides input/output stream objects like cin and cout.

questions.question: What is the size of 'int' data type in C++ (on most systems)?
questions.explanation: On most modern systems, an int is 4 bytes (32 bits), though this can vary by platform.

questions.question: Which operator is used to access members of a namespace in C++?
questions.explanation: The scope resolution operator (::) is used to access members of a namespace or class.

questions.question: What is the correct way to create a comment in C++?
questions.explanation: Single-line comments in C++ start with //. Multi-line comments use /* */.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid C++ data type?
questions.explanation: In C++, the boolean data type is 'bool', not 'boolean'.

questions.question: What does the 'using namespace std;' statement do?
options: Allows using standard library names without std:: prefix
questions.explanation: This statement allows you to use standard library names (like cout, cin) without the std:: prefix.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to terminate a statement in C++?
questions.explanation: Every statement in C++ must end with a semicolon (;).

questions.question: What is the correct way to declare a constant in C++?
questions.explanation: Both 'const int x' and 'int const x' are valid ways to declare a constant in C++.
